mission.title: Install and Configure Kyverno (Policy Engine)
mission.description: Kyverno is a Kubernetes-native policy engine that allows users to manage policies as code for security, compliance, and governance. It is particularly useful for platform engineering teams looking to enforce policies across their Kubernetes clusters. Key trade-offs include the need for a learning curve to effectively write and manage policies, but it integrates seamlessly with Kubernetes workflows.

steps.title: Add the Kyverno Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Kyverno Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add kyverno https://kyverno.github.io/kyverno

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Kyverno repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Kyverno with Helm
steps.description:
This step installs Kyverno in the 'kyverno' namespace using the specified version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace kyverno

helm install kyverno kyverno/kyverno --namespace kyverno --version 1.17.1

```

> Creates a namespace for Kyverno.
> Installs Kyverno with the specified version.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Kyverno pods are running successfully.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kyverno

```

> Lists all pods in the kyverno namespace to verify the installation.

steps.title: Create an example policy
steps.description:
This step creates a sample policy to demonstrate Kyverno's functionality.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kyverno/kyverno/main/docs/examples/policies/policy-validate-image.yaml

```

> Applies a sample policy that validates container images.

steps.title: Verify the policy
steps.description:
This step checks if the policy has been created successfully.

```bash

kubectl get cpol

```

> Lists all custom policies to verify the applied policy.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kyverno
uninstall.description:
This step removes Kyverno from the cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and policies, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kyverno --namespace kyverno

kubectl delete namespace kyverno

```

> Uninstalls the Kyverno release.
> Deletes the Kyverno namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kyverno
upgrade.description:
This step includes backing up existing policies, upgrading Kyverno, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get cpol -o yaml > kyverno-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kyverno kyverno/kyverno --namespace kyverno --version 1.17.1

kubectl get pods -n kyverno

```

> Backs up existing custom policies to a file.
> Upgrades Kyverno to the specified version.
> Verifies that the Kyverno pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install CRD'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the CRD already exists or there are permission issues.

**Fix:**
Check existing CRDs with 'kubectl get crd' and delete any conflicting CRDs.

troubleshooting.title: Kyverno pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This could be due to misconfiguration in policies or insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kyverno' for details.

troubleshooting.title: Policy not enforcing as expected
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The policy might not be applied correctly or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Verify the policy with 'kubectl get cpol <policy-name> -o yaml' and check for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Helm install fails with 'chart not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Helm repository is not added or updated.

**Fix:**
Run 'helm repo update' to refresh the repository cache.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new policy types and enhanced validation features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Kyverno pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n kyverno'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.25 or higher and have kubectl and Helm installed.
